content.text: I found MudAutocomplete has DebounceInterval but no elapsed callback, while MudDebouncedInput implements the existing pattern. Next I’ll inspect the autocomplete implementation and nearby tests to mirror the established behavior.

502	        /// Displays the search result drop-down.
503	        /// </summary>
504	        /// <remarks>
505	        /// When this property changes, the <see cref="OpenChanged"/> event will occur.
506	        /// </remarks>
507	        public bool Open
508	        {
509	            get => _open;
510	            // Note: the setter is protected because it was needed by a user who derived his own autocomplete from this class.
511	            // Note: setting Open will not open or close it. Use ToggleAsync() for that.
512	            protected set
513	            {
514	                if (_open == value)
515	                    return;
516	                _open = value;
517	
518	                OpenChanged.InvokeAsync(_open).CatchAndLog();
519	            }
520	        }
521	
522	        private bool IsLoading => _currentSearchTask is { IsCompleted: false };
523	
524	        private string CurrentIcon
525	        {
526	            get
527	            {
528	                if (!string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(AdornmentIcon))
529	                {
530	                    return AdornmentIcon;
531	                }
532	
533	                return _open ? CloseIcon : OpenIcon;
534	            }
535	        }
536	
537	        /// <summary>
538	        /// Returns a value for the <c>autocomplete</c> attribute, either supplied by default or the one specified in the attribute overrides.

540	        protected object? GetAutocomplete() => UserAttributes.GetValueOrDefault("autocomplete", "off");
541	
542	        public MudAutocomplete()
543	        {
544	            Adornment = Adornment.End;
545	            IconSize = Size.Medium;
546	            Immediate = true;
547	        }
548	
549	        /// <summary>
550	        /// Changes the currently selected item to the specified value.
551	        /// </summary>
552	        /// <param name="value">The value to set.</param>
553	        public async Task SelectOptionAsync(T value)
554	        {
555	            _isProcessingValue = true;
556	            try
557	            {
558	                // #1 needs to close before SetValueAndUpdateTextAsync so that whatever the user puts in ValueChanged can run without the popover being in front of it
559	                // #2 Use "Open" field instead of property to prevent raising multiple OpenChanged events while selecting item.
560	                _open = false;
561	
562	                await SetValueAndUpdateTextAsync(value);
563	
564	                // needs to be open to run the rest of the code
565	                _open = true;
566	
567	                if (_items != null)
568	                    _selectedListItemIndex = Array.IndexOf(_items, value);
569	
570	                var optionText = GetItemString(value);
571	
572	                if (!_isCleared)
573	                    await SetTextAndUpdateValueAsync(optionText, false);
574	
575	                _debounceTimer?.Dispose();
576	
577	                await BeginValidateAsync();
578	
579	                if (!_isCleared)
580	                {
581	                    await _elementReference.SetText(optionText);
582	                }
583	
584	                Open = false;
585	                StateHasChanged();
586	            }
587	            finally
588	            {
589	                _isProcessingValue = false;
590	            }
591	        }
592	
593	        protected override async Task OnInitializedAsync()
594	        {
595	            await base.OnInitializedAsync();
596	            var text = GetItemString(ReadValue);
597	            if (!string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(text))
598	            {
599	                await SetTextCoreAsync(text);
600	            }
601	        }
602	
603	        protected override void OnAfterRender(bool firstRender)
604	        {
605	            if (_isClearing || _isProcessingValue)
606	            {
607	                //When you select a value in the popover, SelectOptionAsync will be called.
608	                //When it reaches SetValueAsync, it will be awaited.
609	                //Meanwhile, in parallel, the ClearAsync method will be called, which sets isCleared to true.
610	                //However, by the time SetValueAsync is released and SelectOptionAsync continues its execution, an OnAfterRender event might fire, setting isCleared back to false.
611	                //This can result in a race condition.
612	                //https://github.com/MudBlazor/MudBlazor/pull/6701
613	                base.OnAfterRender(firstRender);
614	                return;
615	            }
616	
617	            _isCleared = false;
618	
619	            base.OnAfterRender(firstRender);
620	        }
621	
622	        protected override Task UpdateTextPropertyAsync(bool updateValue)
623	        {
624	            // This keeps the text from being set when ClearAsync() was called
625	            if (_isCleared)
626	                return Task.CompletedTask;
627	
628	            return base.UpdateTextPropertyAsync(updateValue);
629	        }
630	
631	        protected override async Task UpdateValuePropertyAsync(bool updateText)
632	        {
633	            _debounceTimer?.Dispose();
634	
635	            if (ResetValueOnEmptyText && string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(ReadText))
636	                await SetValueAndUpdateTextAsync(default(T), updateText);
637	            else if (Immediate)
638	                await CoerceValueToTextAsync();
639	
640	            if (DebounceInterval <= 0)
641	                await OpenMenuAsync();
642	            else
643	                _debounceTimer = TimeProvider.CreateTimer(OnDebounceComplete, null, TimeSpan.FromMilliseconds(DebounceInterval), Timeout.InfiniteTimeSpan);
644	        }
645	
646	        private void OnDebounceComplete(object? stateInfo) => InvokeAsync(OpenMenuAsync);
647	
648	        private void CancelToken()
649	        {
650	            try
651	            {
652	                _cancellationTokenSrc?.Cancel();
653	            }
654	            catch { /*ignored*/ }
655	            finally
656	            {
657	                _cancellationTokenSrc = new CancellationTokenSource();
658	            }
659	        }
660	
661	        private Task SetReturnedItemsCountAsync(int value)
662	        {
663	            _returnedItemsCount = value;
664	            return ReturnedItemsCountChanged.InvokeAsync(value);
665	        }
666	
667	        /// <summary>
668	        /// Opens or closes the drop-down of items depending on whether it was closed or open.
669	        /// </summary>
670	        /// <remarks>
671	        /// Will have no effect if the autocomplete is disabled or read-only.
672	        /// </remarks>
673	        public Task ToggleMenuAsync()
674	        {
675	            if (!Open && (GetDisabledState() || GetReadOnlyState()))
676	            {
677	                return Task.CompletedTask;
678	            }
679	
680	            return Open ? CloseMenuAsync() : OpenMenuAsync();
681	        }
682	
683	        /// <summary>
684	        /// Closes the drop-down of items.
685	        /// </summary>
686	        public async Task CloseMenuAsync()
687	        {
688	            CancelToken();
689	            _debounceTimer?.Dispose();

content.text: I found MudAutocomplete has its own timer-based DebounceInterval implementation but lacks the OnDebounceIntervalElapsed callback that MudDebouncedInput provides. I’m checking test patterns and component internals next so I can add a focused regression test before implementing the fix.

1	using Microsoft.AspNetCore.Components;
2	using Microsoft.AspNetCore.Components.Web;
3	using Microsoft.Extensions.Logging;
4	using MudBlazor.Utilities;

6	namespace MudBlazor
7	{
8	    /// <summary>
9	    /// A text input for searching and selecting from a list of options. Unlike <see cref="MudSelect{T}"/>, it doesn't require the complete item list upfront and supports asynchronous search for database queries.
10	    /// </summary>
11	    /// <typeparam name="T">The type of item to search.</typeparam>
12	    /// <seealso cref="MudSelect{T}"/>
13	    public partial class MudAutocomplete<T> : MudBaseInput<T>
14	    {
15	        /// <summary>
16	        /// We need a random id for the year items in the year list so we can scroll to the item safely in every DatePicker.
17	        /// </summary>
18	        private readonly string _componentId = Identifier.Create();
19	
20	        private bool _isCleared;
21	        private bool _isClearing;
22	        private bool _isProcessingValue;
23	        private int _selectedListItemIndex;
24	        private readonly int _elementKey = 0;
25	        private int _returnedItemsCount;
26	        private bool _open;
27	        private bool _opening;
28	        private bool _isValueCoerced;
29	        private MudInput<string> _elementReference = null!;
30	        private CancellationTokenSource? _cancellationTokenSrc;
31	        private Task? _currentSearchTask;
32	        private ITimer? _debounceTimer;
33	        private T[]? _items;
34	        private List<int> _enabledItemIndices = [];
35	        private bool _handleNextFocus;
36	
37	        [Inject]
38	        private IScrollManager ScrollManager { get; set; } = null!;
39	
40	        [Inject]
41	        private IPopoverService PopoverService { get; set; } = null!;
42	
43	        [Inject]
44	        private TimeProvider TimeProvider { get; set; } = null!;
45	
46	        protected string Classname =>
47	            new CssBuilder("mud-select")
48	                .AddClass(Class)
49	                .Build();
50	
51	        protected string InputClassname =>
52	            new CssBuilder("mud-select-input")
53	                .AddClass("mud-readonly", GetReadOnlyState())
54	                .AddClass(InputClass)
55	                .Build();
56	
57	        protected string AutocompleteClassname =>
58	            new CssBuilder("mud-select")
59	                .AddClass("mud-autocomplete")
60	                .AddClass("mud-width-full", FullWidth)
61	                .AddClass("mud-autocomplete--with-progress", ShowProgressIndicator && IsLoading)
62	                .Build();
63	
64	        protected string CircularProgressClassname =>
65	            new CssBuilder("progress-indicator-circular")
66	                .AddClass("progress-indicator-circular--with-adornment", Adornment == Adornment.End)
67	                .Build();
68	
69	        protected string GetListItemClassname(bool isSelected) =>
70	            new CssBuilder()
71	                .AddClass("mud-selected-item mud-primary-text mud-primary-hover", isSelected)
72	                .AddClass(ListItemClass)
73	                .Build();

content.text: I’ve identified MudAutocomplete as the component missing the event and am checking existing fake-time test patterns so I can add a focused regression test first.

content.text:
Implemented the fix in `src/MudBlazor/Components/Autocomplete/MudAutocomplete.razor.cs`.

Summary:
- Added `OnDebounceIntervalElapsed` parameter to `MudAutocomplete<T>`.
- Invokes `OnDebounceIntervalElapsed` with the current `ReadText` when the autocomplete debounce timer completes.
- Verified only `MudAutocomplete` and `MudDebouncedInput` expose `DebounceInterval`, and both now support `OnDebounceIntervalElapsed`.

Validation:
- Added a temporary regression test confirming the event fires after the debounce interval; it failed before the fix and passed after.
- Removed the temporary test afterward per instructions.
- Ran existing autocomplete debounce-related tests successfully:
  - `OnTextChanged_CoerceValueAndNotCoerceTextAndImmediateAndDebounce_SetValueImmediatelyButDelaysMenuOpening`
  - `OnTextChanged_WithCoerceValueAndNotCoerceTextAndImmediateNotDebounce_SetValueAndOpenMenuImmediately`

Working tree currently has only the source fix modified:
- `src/MudBlazor/Components/Autocomplete/MudAutocomplete.razor.cs`

result:
